_comment: GENERATED by tools/leiden_receipt.py — do not edit by hand. Machine-readable map of Sophia mechanisms to the five Leiden values.
declaration: Leiden Declaration on Artificial Intelligence and Mathematics (2026); https://leidendeclaration.ai/

values.name: Proof & certainty
values.principle: Results carry the highest justified degree of certainty.
values.sophia_form: Fail-closed verifier gate (accept/abstain/block) plus the Instrumented Evaluation Contract: CIs, pre-registered MDE, and anytime-valid confidence sequences before any number backs a claim.

values.name: Attribution & responsibility
values.principle: Results are attributable to specific humans who take responsibility for their correctness; credit is not given to automated systems.
values.sophia_form: canClaimAGI:false throughout; a generated Tool & Computational Resource Disclosure; a no-AI-authorship lint; and abstention when provenance cannot be established.

values.name: Transparency & independent verifiability
values.principle: Arguments are transparent and subject to independent verification.
values.sophia_form: Public failure ledger of what is NOT proven, aggregates-only results published from a single source of truth, and a clean-clone replication checklist.

values.name: Shared standards of evaluation
values.principle: Work is evaluated by collectively established criteria.
values.sophia_form: The Instrumented Evaluation Contract (8 pillars) and the PROTECTED per-domain standards (religion/history) are published for reuse; external adoption and more non-self-authored benchmarks remain open work.

values.name: Autonomous direction & non-proprietary tooling
values.principle: Humans shape research directions; the field favors non-proprietary, publicly governed tools.
values.sophia_form: Local-first, Apache-2.0, runs on owned hardware. A self-hostable open-weights judge backend (agent/open_judge.py) and a judge-openness registry (agent/judge_registry.py) now exist; the value is fully met once a headline result is corroborated on a non-proprietary path (see open_gaps: open_model_judge_family).

open_gaps.summary: Add an open-weights judge family so the >=2-judge-family validation rule no longer depends on proprietary inference services.
open_gaps.note: Registry (agent/judge_registry.py) + self-hostable backend (agent/open_judge.py) landed; remaining: corroborate a headline result on a non-proprietary path and record judge openness in its receipt.

open_gaps.summary: Add a formal-proof (e.g. Lean) verifier tier so formal/mathematical claims must carry a machine-checked artifact or be labeled informal-unverified.
Drive the hidden-reviewer / clean-clone external replication path to reduce reliance on self-authored benchmarks.
